core_takeaway: The first position beat the last position by securing Apple while the last position accepted the residual bundle, but the biggest winner was the middle speaker, Agent_2, who captured Pencil. The first-position advantage looks real but indirect: Agent_1 helped frame Apple as a fair high-value consolation and Agent_3 repeatedly legitimized a 23-point residual bundle as acceptable.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 finished with 33 points and Agent_3 with 23, so the first speaker did better than the last. The advantage was not dominance over the whole bargain, since Agent_2 got the 44-point Pencil. Instead, Agent_1 benefited because the public discussion and private votes converged on Baseline B: Apple to Agent_1, Pencil to Agent_2, and the lower three items to Agent_3.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by making Pencil and Apple the central items, then proposed a version giving Agent_1 Pencil and Agent_3 the three-item residual. Agent_2 displaced that with Baseline B, and Agent_3 endorsed it because 23 points was its strongest immediate payoff among the offers it considered.
phase_notes.private_thinking: All three private-thinking entries accepted Baseline B as the practical path. Agent_1 privately favored securing Apple while leaving Pencil with Agent_2; Agent_2 focused on guarding Pencil; Agent_3 treated its 23-point residual bundle as enough to keep momentum.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Every formal proposal used the same substantive allocation: Agent_1 gets Apple, Agent_2 gets Pencil, and Agent_3 gets Jewel, Stone, and Quill. The proposal order difference in Agent_3's bundle was immaterial.
phase_notes.private_voting: All nine private votes accepted. Agent_2 accepted because Pencil was secured, Agent_1 accepted because Apple plus future renegotiation was adequate, and Agent_3 accepted because 23 points now was stable and avoided risk.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection entries were present in the transcript.

mechanisms.theme: Apple became the first speaker's accepted consolation prize
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_2's early offer took Pencil but explicitly gave Apple to Agent_1, turning the second-best item into a stable fairness anchor for the first speaker. That gave Agent_1 a 33-point floor while leaving Agent_3 with the lower-value remainder.

evidence.quote: Offer 1 (conservative, clear): I take Pencil; you get Apple; Agent_3 gets Stone, Quill, and Jewel.
Apple goes to Agent_1, preserving a strong second priority for you.

mechanisms.theme: The last speaker normalized taking the residual bundle
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_3 repeatedly described the three-item remainder as acceptable or preferred among available baselines. This reduced pressure to give the last position Apple or Pencil and made the 23-point outcome self-ratifying.

evidence.quote: My preferred baseline: I support adopting Offer 1 as Round 1.
I’m on board with Baseline B as Round 1.
It yields Agent_3 a total of 23 now

mechanisms.theme: Future renegotiation language made the unequal first round easier to accept
mechanisms.explanation: The bargain repeatedly framed Baseline B as only a first-round starting point with possible later adjustments. That helped all agents accept a final allocation where Agent_1 already beat Agent_3, even though no later round occurred in the transcript.

evidence.quote: Round 2 should explicitly aim to improve overall balance
leaves a clear path for two-round renegotiation

mechanisms.theme: Formal convergence locked in first over last
mechanisms.explanation: Once proposals were submitted, all three carried the same substantive split. The formal enumeration shows Agent_1 with Apple and Agent_3 with Jewel, Stone, and Quill; the vote tabulation then made that allocation unanimous.

mechanisms.theme: Middle-position capture of Pencil limits the first-advantage story
mechanisms.explanation: The strongest asset went to Agent_2, not Agent_1. This is a counterexample to a simple first-mover interpretation: Agent_1 beat the last speaker, but the middle speaker used its turn to make Pencil ownership the agreed anchor.

evidence.quote: baseline B already places Pencil with Agent_2
